plan_title: Launch a SaaS MVP in 90 Days
plan_text: Ship a working SaaS product with paying customers by end of Q3. Week 1–2: Finalize requirements. Week 3–8: Core dev. Week 9–10: QA. Week 11: Beta. Week 12: Launch.

user_pov.thought_getting: A fast, focused product they can rely on from day one
user_pov.would_forgive: Rough edges in the UI if core features work reliably
user_pov.untrustworthy: If billing breaks or data disappears — trust is gone instantly
user_pov.predictable_complaints: Why is onboarding so confusing? Where are the docs? Why did my export fail?

personas.name: Skeptical-Money
personas.rationale: Budget is tight and runway is short — every dollar matters

personas.name: Frustrated-End-User
personas.rationale: Beta users will judge fast; bad UX kills word-of-mouth

personas.name: Change-Resister
personas.rationale: Internal stakeholders may block scope decisions

personas.name: Ops-Engineer
personas.rationale: Solo engineer means no ops redundancy

personas.name: AI-Optimism Auditor
personas.rationale: Plan uses suspiciously round numbers and confident language

Skeptical-Money.3mo: Burn rate hit 140% of budget by week 6. The Stripe integration took 3 weeks, not 1. Investor is asking hard questions.
Skeptical-Money.6mo: Cash reserves are at 2 months. Customer acquisition cost is 4x the forecast. The pivot conversation has started.
Skeptical-Money.12mo: Company shut down in month 9. Post-mortem cited underestimated infrastructure costs and overestimated conversion rates.

Frustrated-End-User.3mo: Onboarding takes 40 minutes. Three of the 10 beta users churned in week 1. The ones who stayed are frustrated but loyal.
Frustrated-End-User.6mo: Feature requests are piling up. Users built workarounds. The product feels like it was built for the demo, not for daily use.
Frustrated-End-User.12mo: NPS dropped to 12. The core users who stuck around feel ignored. Two switched to a competitor with better UX.

Change-Resister.3mo: The founding engineer is making all architecture decisions alone. No code review, no documentation.
Change-Resister.6mo: New hire can't onboard. The codebase is a maze. Technical debt is blocking feature work.
Change-Resister.12mo: The original engineer burned out and left. The replacement team is rewriting from scratch.

Ops-Engineer.3mo: No monitoring. First outage was 4 hours long, discovered by a customer tweet.
Ops-Engineer.6mo: Database hit connection limits during a press mention spike. No auto-scaling. Manual recovery took 6 hours.
Ops-Engineer.12mo: Third major outage triggered a contractual SLA breach with the largest customer.

Competitor-Strategist.3mo: Competitor launched a free tier 2 weeks after our beta. Our unique differentiator is already commoditized.
Competitor-Strategist.6mo: Competitor is outspending us 10:1 on marketing. Our beta users are getting poached with migration tools.
Competitor-Strategist.12mo: We're a footnote in their case study about how they won the segment.

AI-Optimism Auditor.3mo: The '10 paying customers on day 1' target was a round number, not derived from pipeline data. Actual conversion: 3.
AI-Optimism Auditor.6mo: NPS ≥ 30 was aspirational, not baselined. Actual NPS: 18. The plan never defined what 'paying' meant (monthly? annual?).
AI-Optimism Auditor.12mo: Every success metric in the original plan was unfalsifiable. The team couldn't tell if they succeeded or failed.

confirmed_risks.name: Stripe integration timeline underestimated
confirmed_risks.tripwire: Stripe integration not code-complete by end of week 3
confirmed_risks.change_my_mind: Prior Stripe integration experience on this exact stack
confirmed_risks.mitigation_summary: Start Stripe integration in week 1, not week 3

confirmed_risks.name: Single engineer as single point of failure
confirmed_risks.tripwire: Engineer works >60 hours in any 2-week period
confirmed_risks.change_my_mind: Second engineer hired and onboarded before week 4
confirmed_risks.mitigation_summary: Hire a part-time contractor for ops and QA

confirmed_risks.name: No monitoring or incident response
confirmed_risks.tripwire: First customer-reported outage with no internal alert
confirmed_risks.change_my_mind: Monitoring stack deployed before beta launch
confirmed_risks.mitigation_summary: Deploy basic uptime monitoring and alerting before beta
citations: Ops-Engineer: 'First outage was 4 hours, discovered by tweet'

confirmed_risks.name: Beta user churn from poor onboarding
confirmed_risks.tripwire: More than 2 of 10 beta users churn in first 2 weeks
confirmed_risks.change_my_mind: Dedicated onboarding flow tested with 3 users before beta
confirmed_risks.mitigation_summary: Build guided onboarding wizard before beta launch

confirmed_risks.name: Success metrics are unfalsifiable
confirmed_risks.tripwire: Team cannot agree on whether Q3 was a success or failure
confirmed_risks.change_my_mind: Metrics redefined with baselines and measurement methodology before week 2
confirmed_risks.mitigation_summary: Redefine all success metrics with baselines and measurement plans

inflated_risks.name: Competitor launches free tier
inflated_risks.why_downgraded: Free tiers rarely convert enterprise buyers. Our 10 beta users are hand-picked design partners, not price-sensitive prospects. The competitive threat is real at 12mo but inflated at 3mo.

inflated_risks.name: Database scaling limits
inflated_risks.why_downgraded: At 10 users, database load is negligible. This becomes real only if the launch drives unexpected viral traffic, which the plan does not forecast.

buried_risks.name: Founder burnout
buried_risks.why_unspoken: The founding engineer is also the CEO. Nobody in the room will tell them they're overcommitted.
buried_risks.how_to_surface: Ask: 'What does a sustainable week look like for you in month 3?'

buried_risks.name: Investor patience is shorter than stated
buried_risks.why_unspoken: The investor said '90 days' but their actual threshold is 'visible traction by week 6'. Nobody wants to name the real deadline.
buried_risks.how_to_surface: Ask the investor directly: 'What would make you uncomfortable at week 6?'

agreements: All personas agree: the Stripe timeline is the highest-risk single item
Skeptical-Money and AI-Optimism Auditor converge on unfalsifiable metrics
Ops-Engineer and Change-Resister agree that single-engineer risk is severe
disagreements: Frustrated-End-User wants onboarding investment now; Skeptical-Money says ship fast and iterate
Competitor-Strategist rates competitive threat as urgent; AI-Optimism Auditor says the threat is inflated at this stage
cascades: Stripe delay → billing not ready → can't charge beta users → '10 paying customers' metric fails. Severity uplift: Critical
Engineer burnout → quality drops → outages increase → beta users churn → investor loses confidence. Severity uplift: High

mitigations.action: Start Stripe integration in week 1, not week 3. Allocate 2 full weeks.
Hire a part-time contractor for ops and QA by week 3
Deploy Uptime Robot + PagerDuty before beta launch
Build and user-test a 3-step onboarding wizard
Redefine success metrics: 'paying' = monthly subscriber; NPS baseline from beta week 1

revised_plan:
Ship a working SaaS product with paying customers by end of Q3.

Week 1: Start Stripe integration + redefine success metrics with baselines
Week 2: Stripe integration complete + hire ops contractor posted
Week 3–8: Core dev (contractor handles ops/QA from week 3)
Week 9: Onboarding wizard built + monitoring deployed
Week 10: QA sprint
Week 11: Beta with 10 design-partner customers
Week 12: Public launch

_[Mitigation for Risk #1: Stripe moved to week 1]_
_[Mitigation for Risk #2: Contractor hired by week 3]_
_[Mitigation for Risk #3: Monitoring before beta]_
_[Mitigation for Risk #4: Onboarding wizard before beta]_
_[Mitigation for Risk #5: Metrics redefined in week 1]_

evidence.title: Color Labs shutdown (90-day SaaS failure)
evidence.what_happened: Launched MVP in 90 days with single engineer. Billing integration delayed 3 weeks. First paying customer arrived on day 40, not day 1.
evidence.why_relevant: Nearly identical timeline and team structure. Same Stripe underestimation pattern.

evidence.title: Basecamp's 'Shape Up' on timeline risk
evidence.what_happened: Documented that 6-week cycles fail when scope isn't fixed. Betting table process introduced to force scope cuts.
evidence.why_relevant: The 90-day plan has no scope-cut mechanism. When timeline slips, everything slips.

evidence.title: Startup founder burnout study (Gallup)
evidence.what_happened: 45% of solo technical founders reported burnout symptoms by month 3. Burnout correlated with >55 hour weeks sustained for >4 weeks.
evidence.why_relevant: The plan assumes one engineer carries backend solo for 12 weeks. This matches the burnout risk profile exactly.
